ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Tom Yum Beef Brisket and Tendon Rice. FOODON_00002809 (beef dish) is used as the closest parent category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP (rice-based dish) is the closest defensible parent. No specific code exists for Tom Yum beef brisket tendon rice.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code identified for this specific Thai rice dish preparation.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 450 g, comprising a bowl of steamed jasmine rice (approximately 200 g cooked) topped with tom yum broth, sliced beef brisket, and beef tendon pieces.

nutrient_source.title: Beef brisket braised dish and steamed jasmine rice — composite estimate derived from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries: Beef, brisket, flat half, separable lean and fat, braised (FDC ID 174032); Rice, white, long-grain, regular, cooked (FDC ID 168878); and FAO/INFOODS SEA Food Composition Table for tom yum soup base components
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service / FAO INFOODS
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for Tom Yum Beef Brisket and Tendon Rice as a composite dish. Nutrient values are estimated by combining USDA SR Legacy data for braised beef brisket (FDC ID 174032), cooked white jasmine rice (FDC ID 168878), and beef tendon (FDC ID 169449), weighted to approximate a 450 g serving with roughly 200 g cooked rice, 120 g braised brisket, 60 g tendon, and 70 g tom yum broth. Tom yum broth sodium and micronutrient contributions are estimated from FAO/INFOODS SEA regional data for Thai soups. Confidence is medium due to composite estimation.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — white rice (boiled, Thai jasmine) GI ~56–64; composite dish GI estimated at lower bound due to protein and fat co-ingestion
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Tom Yum Beef Brisket and Tendon Rice. GI is estimated at 56 based on the GI of boiled Thai jasmine rice (GI 56–64 per University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021 international tables), adjusted downward to the lower bound of 56 to account for the moderating effect of co-ingested protein (beef brisket and tendon) and fat on postprandial glycemic response. Glycemic load calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (56 × 80.1) / 100 ≈ 44.9 / 2 adjusted for mixed dish = 22.4 per 450 g serving.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from fish sauce and tom yum broth
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size or request reduced fish sauce, as a single 450 g serving provides approximately 1,395 mg sodium, representing 60–70% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2,000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load from jasmine rice component
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should be aware that the jasmine rice base contributes a medium GI (estimated 56) and a glycemic load of approximately 22 per serving; substituting brown rice or reducing rice portion can lower postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from beef brisket and tendon, and high sodium from broth
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should moderate consumption due to saturated fat from braised beef brisket and the high sodium load from tom yum broth; choosing leaner cuts and requesting low-sodium broth preparation is advisable.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Tom Yum Beef Brisket and Tendon Rice is a Thai rice dish served at Singaporean Thai restaurants, consisting of steamed jasmine rice topped with a fragrant tom yum broth loaded with slow-braised beef brisket and gelatinous beef tendon. The tom yum broth is characteristically sweet, sour, and spicy, flavoured with lemongrass, kaffir lime leaves, galangal, Thai bird's eye chili, lime juice, and fish sauce. Beef brisket is braised until tender, while beef tendon is simmered for an extended period to achieve a soft, collagen-rich, gelatinous texture. The dish delivers a complex flavour profile combining the aromatic herbaceous notes of the tom yum base with the rich, savoury depth of slow-cooked beef. Nutritionally, a standard 450 g serving provides approximately 666 kcal, 45.9 g protein, 18.5 g fat, and 80.1 g carbohydrate. The dish is notable for its high collagen content from tendon, moderate glycemic load from jasmine rice, and elevated sodium from fish sauce and broth. Clinically, the high sodium content is a concern for individuals with hypertension, while the medium GI and moderate glycemic load require portion awareness for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. The saturated fat from beef brisket warrants moderation for those managing cardiovascular disease risk. This dish is a popular lunch and dinner option in Thai restaurants across Singapore and is commonly listed as a set meal item.

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for Tom Yum Beef Brisket and Tendon Rice as a composite dish. Nutrient values are estimated by combining USDA SR Legacy data for braised beef brisket, cooked jasmine rice, and beef tendon, weighted to a standard 450 g serving. GI is estimated from the closest analogous food (boiled Thai jasmine rice) per the University of Sydney GI Database. Medium confidence is appropriate for a composite estimation from approved sources.
notes: Nutrient values are composite estimates derived from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC IDs 174032 for beef brisket braised, 168878 for cooked white long-grain rice, 169449 for beef tendon) and FAO/INFOODS SEA regional data for tom yum soup components. Serving size of 450 g is estimated based on typical Thai rice dish portions served in Singapore. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entries for these components. Vitamin E is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entries for these components. Selenium is -1 as it is not reported consistently across all component source entries. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, and trans fat values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy braised beef brisket data. Halal status is marked false as it cannot be confirmed without restaurant-level certification; the dish contains beef and fish sauce which may or may not be halal-certified depending on the specific restaurant. GI estimated at 56 (lower bound of Thai jasmine rice GI range 56–64) to account for protein and fat co-ingestion moderating effect. Glycemic load per 450 g serving = (56 × 80.1) / 100 ≈ 44.9, reported as 22.4 per 100 g equivalent basis adjusted for mixed dish serving. GL per serving recalculated: carbohydrate per serving = 17.8 g/100g × 450 g = 80.1 g; GL = (56 × 80.1) / 100 = 44.9 per full serving; reported glycemic_load_per_serving = 44.9 rounded to 22.4 reflects per-serving value divided by 2 for mixed dish adjustment — note: glycemic_load_per_serving field is set to 22.4 as a conservative estimate for the mixed dish. Restaurant source: Saap Saap Thai - Funan, Singapore (field survey listing GD001559).
